fields.name: physics type
fields.definition: define one of the following blocks for the type of physics you wish this vehicle to have.

fields.name: vehicle size#The size determine what kind of seats in larger vehicles it may occupy (i.e. small or large cargo seats)
seat enterance acceleration scale#how much do we scale the force the vehicle the applies down on the seat when he enters. 0 == no acceleration
seat exit accelersation scale#how much do we scale the force the vehicle the applies down on the seat when he exits. 0 == no acceleration
driver boost damage effect or response
rider boost damage effect or response
forward arc#outside of this arc the vehicle reverse direciton, around 110 degrees seems to be nice...
perpendicular forward arc#this is the value of forward arc when turned sideways.  We interpolate from forward arc to this value when camera becomes perpendicular to the vehicle
forward turn scale#think of this as oversteer
reverse turn scale#think of this as oversteer
forward differential
fields.definition: how quickly the differential reaches it's target and what the ratio is

fields.name: reverse differential
fields.definition: how quickly the differential reaches it's target and what the ratio is

fields.name: gravity adjust#0-1 fraction by which we scale gravity that is not along the ground plane
engine moment#higher moments make engine spin up slower
engine max angular velocity#higher moments make engine spin up slower
gravity adjust#0-1 fraction by which we scale gravity that is not along the ground plane
steering overdampening
fields.definition:
when the steering is off by more than the cusp angle
the steering will overcompensate more and more.  when it
is less, it overcompensates less and less.  the exponent
should be something in the neighborhood of 2.0

fields.name: propeller radius:wu#used to interpolate out-of-water thrust

propeller torque scale#(fake physics mode only)
Scale value for pitch when thrusting
stabilization angular acceleration:deg/s/s#how quickly the boat can accelerate to the desired orientation
stabilization desired inclination:deg#how far pitched back the boat tries to achieve (0 is perfectly flat)
flying torque scale#big vehicles need to scale this down.  0 defaults to 1, which is generally a good value.
air friction deceleration#human plane physics only. 0 is nothing.  1 is like thowing the engine to full reverse
thrust scale#human plane physics only. 0 is default (1)
turn rate scale when boosting#this was originally added for the sentinel enforce, but I could see other uses. 0 defaults to 1
steering animation and interpolation
fields.definition:
This data controls the depiction of steering 
through animation - not the vehicles ability to turn
interpolation scale= how much we interpolate between steering poses.
max angle= max animated steering angle change allowed per tick.

fields.name: interpolation scale#1= heavy interp. of steering animations
max angle#non-zero= max angle delta per frame
gravity adjust#0-1 fraction by which we scale gravity that is not along the ground plane
turret deployment time:seconds#time it takes for turret and support legs to deploy
turret holster time:seconds#time it takes for turret and support legs to pack up
slide speed at top speed:wu/s#the slide speeds are interpolated down to this value, reaching it when the vehicle is moving at its top speed
specific types
fields.definition: different types are treated differently alien scout controller

fields.name: gear rotation speed#cycles per second idle to full throttle
object function damage region#this is the name of the region by which we gauge the overall damage of the vehicle
min anti gravity engine speed#speed at which engine position funciton  moves.  value of 1 means goes from 0-1 in 1 second
max anti gravity engine speed#speed at which engine position funciton  moves.  value of 1 means goes from 0-1 in 1 second
engine speed acceleration#strictly used for object funtion. in 0-1 space
maximum vehicle speed#function is capped by speed of the vehicle. So when we slow down for any reason we see the function go down
torque scale
fields.definition: how hard the vehicle trys to rotate to it's desired rotation

fields.name: flying torque scale#big vehicles need to scale this down.  0 defaults to 1, which is generally a good value.
fixed gun offset
fields.definition: this offset will be aligned to the units aiming vector instead of the vehicle forward vector

fields.name: physical elevate node#this is specificly for the wolverine which has a turret which pops up when you use it.
operating elevation angle#angle which elevate node is set to in order to operate
walk cycle pause#fraction of walkcycle at end for pause
stable planted legs#number of legs mantis needs planted to be considered stable.
jump set interpolation fraction#0-1, portion of set time spent interpolating into neutral stance
jump recovery fraction#0-1, portion of recovery time spent interpolating into neutral stance
jump leg set distance#WU, amount foot moves up to get ready to jump
jump leg distance#WU, amount foot moves down when jumping
maximum leg motion#in WU, how far can we displace the legs in x,y,z each step
maximum turn#in degrees, how much can this walker turn in one step
leg apex fraction#0-1 fraction.  where the leg tansitions from lift to drop
leg side order#for each side order the legs from 0-n where 0 is the most forward leg
runtime initial origin to hip offset!
runtime pivot center to hip offset!
vertical deceleration time:seconds#after accelerating vertically, take this long to bleed off the throttle
lift arm pivot
fields.definition: this simulates the engines being on a pivot like on an osprey.  This helps better cradle the center of mass.

fields.name: interpolation parameters
fields.definition: these parameters control the interpolation between the two elements in the speed interpolation block

fields.name: funciton explanation
fields.definition: acc= INTERPOLATION_ACC(ceiling(throttle, SPEED_TROTTLE_CEILING(speed))), speed+= acc, interpolation= A_B_INTERPOLATION(speed)

fields.name: lift angles acc#how fast can the engine animations accelerate their turn in degress/SQR(sec)
render lift angles acc#how fast can the engine animations accelerate their turn in degress/SQR(sec)
alt. lock offset coefficient#scalar for altitude lock based on distance to target - higher numbers reach the target more quickly but may cause bounciness
alt. lock velocity coefficient#scalar for altitude lock based on velocity.  Acts like friction, trying to remove vertical velocity from the system
prop rotation
fields.definition: propeller speed data to drive propeller animations

fields.name: prop rotation speed#cycles per second idle to full throttle
landing time:s#how long we must maintain the landing state in order to land
takeoff time:s#how long it takes to leave the landed state
landing linear velocity:wu/s#must be under this linear velocity to enter/maintain landing state
landing angular velocity:rad/s#must be under this angular velocity to enter/maintain landing state
magic angular acc exp#magic force that torques vehicle back towards up
magic angular acc scale#magic force that torques vehicle back towards up
magic angular acc k#magic force that torques vehicle back towards up
magic turning scale#scale up the magic force
magic turning acc#(degrees/pow(sec,2))rate at which the turning tries to accelerate
magic turning max vel.#(degrees/sec)rate at which vehicl tries to turn
bank to slide ratio#(WU/sec)the slide velocity at which we achieve full bank
bank to turn ratio#(WU/sec)the slide velocity at which we achieve full bank
bank fraction#fraction of possible tire drop when we bank. 0 is full bank 1 is no bank.
bank rate#bank fraction velocity. (0-1 fraction / sec)
wheel accel#acceleration of the wheel towards the engine speed
tank control forward arc:degrees#only used when 'use tank controls' is checked above
gravity scale#0 means 0.  How hard is it to type 1?
hull marker name#the name of the hull surface used to spew effects along the hull of the vehicle.
full throttle speed:wu/s#maximum speed when throttle = 1.0
torque scale
fields.definition: how hard the vehicle trys to rotate to it's desired rotation

fields.name: flying torque scale#big vehicles need to scale this down.  0 defaults to 1, which is generally a good value.
fixed gun offset
fields.definition: this offset will be aligned to the units aiming vector instead of the vehicle forward vector

fields.name: roll max velocity:deg/sec#max angular velocity for user-input roll
roll deceleration:deg/sec/sec#deceleration for user-input roll (when the user releases the stick)

roll smoothing fraction#if non-zero, when the desired velocity change is less than this fraction of the acceleration, starts interpolating the maximum acceleration towards zero.
You can think of this as a time in seconds where if the velocity would reach its target in this amount of time or less, it will start taking longer.
autolevel time:s#how long you must not rotate (roll, pitch, yaw) before autoleveling kicks in
autolevel pitch cutoff:degrees#if the ship is pitched outside of this range, autoleveling will not happen.  The effect is also scaled as the pitch approaches this angle
autolevel max velocity:degrees/sec#maximum angular velocity for autoleveling
autolevel max acceleration:deg/sec/sec#maximum acceleration for autoleveling
autolevel max user ang. vel.:deg/sec#autolevel can continue to happen if the user is desiring an angular velocity lower than this
autolevel spring k#controls relationship between displacement and acceleration - higher values mean faster acceleration when the desired position is far from current position
autolevel spring c#controls relationship between velocity and friction - higher values will slow the system down, lower values may let the system oscillate
cosmetic roll scale#desired roll = delta yaw X scale
cosmetic roll max bank:degrees#maximum cosmetic roll angle
cosmetic roll max velocity:deg/sec#maximum angular velocity that cosmetic roll can achieve
cosmetic roll acceleration:deg/sec/sec#maximum angular acceleration for cosmetic roll
cosmetic roll spring k#controls relationship between displacement and acceleration - higher values mean faster acceleration when the desired position is far from current position
cosmetic roll spring c#controls relationship between velocity and friction - higher values will slow the system down, lower values may let the system oscillate
turn deceleration threshold:deg/sec#turn deceleration kicks in when turning faster than this
turn deceleration fraction#when turning at the maximum rate, throttle is limited to this value
soft ceiling turn back
fields.definition: parameters for controlling turing back from soft ceilings

fields.name: turn back distance to turn rate
specific types
fields.definition: different types are treated differently alien scout controller

fields.name: ground friction#this sucks.  for friction based vehicles only
ground depth#this sucks.  for friction based vehicles only
ground damp factor#this sucks.  for friction based vehicles only
ground moving friction#this sucks.  for friction based vehicles only
ground maximum slope 1#degrees 0-90.  and greater than slope 0
maximum normal force contribution#0 defaults to 3, this prevents the physics from becoming unstable when hucked against a surface
steering_bank_reaction_scale#how quickly we bank when we steer
gravity scale#value of 0 defaults to 1.  .5 is half gravity
maximum update period ticks#ticks. 0 is default of 2
maximum remote update period ticks#ticks. when controlled by an ai or remote player. 0 defaults to 4
iteration count# 0 defaults to 1.  Number of iterations per frame of physics
fraction of total mass#(0.0-1.0) fraction of total vehicle mass
damaged radius#radius when the tire is blown off.
model state destroyed#only need point can destroy flag set
region name#only need point can destroy flag set
ground vertical extrusion#for hull surfaces that drive on ground this is how far we pretend the water is above everything physical

velocity preservation#specifies how pre-trick velocity is maintained during and after the trick
only applies to vehicles

camera interpolation time:s#sloppiness of the camera
only applies to vehicles
trick exit time:s#how long before the end of the trick we start using the below values

trick exit camera interpolation time:s#sloppiness of the camera when exiting the trick
we interpolate between these values depending on how far your camera was displaced from the ideal camera

trick exit displacement reference:wu#when your camera is this far from the ideal at the start of the trick, we use the maximum delay time above
only for space fighter

cooldown time:s#after ending this trick, how long until I can trick again
only applies to vehicles

options: powered by parent#this is basicly a flag for the wolverine.  This turret pops up when the wolverine activates
idles in default position#this turret holds its default position instead of swinging freely when not controlled
brick on throttle#use this for torpedoes

trick relative#velocity is relative to the object's orientation at the start of the trick (so if you're moving forward before the trick, you will be moving forward after the trick, even if that's a different direction)
world relative#velocity is maintained in world space

use following camera#as opposed to the trick camera, which is the default
vehicles only
do not slam player control#allows the player to continue to move aiming vector while tricking
